I have a Blazor Web App with Interactive Server (per-page).

I'm building a company intranet. I need ASP.NET Core Identity with
role-based access control. Here's what I need:

1. Set up Identity with three roles: Admin, Manager, and Employee.
   Use an in-memory database for now. Seed one test user per role
   on startup so I can test everything:
   - admin@company.com (Admin role)
   - manager@company.com (Manager role)
   - employee@company.com (Employee role)
   All with password "Test123!"

2. An interactive /admin/users page. Only Admins should be able to
   access this page. Show a list of all users with their roles
   (query from UserManager).

3. An interactive /reports page that both Admins and Managers can access
   but not regular Employees. Show some placeholder report content.

4. An interactive /directory page accessible to any authenticated user.
   Show a simple employee list.

5. The navigation menu should show or hide links based on the current
   user's roles — don't show the admin link to non-admins, don't show
   reports to employees.

6. If an unauthenticated user tries to visit any protected page, they
   should be sent to a login page. If an authenticated user visits a
   page they don't have the role for, they should see an "Access Denied"
   message.

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me plan the implementation:

1. Add NuGet packages: Microsoft.AspNetCore.Identity.EntityFrameworkCore, Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.InMemory
2. Create a DbContext (Data/ApplicationDbContext.cs)
3. Update Program.cs to configure Identity, EF Core in-memory, seed users/roles
4. Create login page (Account/Login.razor) - needs to be a regular page with form post since Identity uses cookies
5. Create access denied page
6. Create the protected pages: /admin/users, /reports, /directory


7. Update the navigation menu to conditionally display links based on user roles, configure the routing to use AuthorizeRouteView, and wrap the app with CascadingAuthenticationState

Since Blazor Server with Identity requires traditional form POST for login and logout due to cookie authentication, I'll keep those as non-interactive pages while the protected content can use interactive server components. I'll need to add the Authorize attribute to restrict access to those pages.

data.content:
Here's a summary of everything I set up:

**Identity & Data**
- `Data/ApplicationDbContext.cs` — EF Core Identity context with in-memory database
- `Program.cs` — Identity configuration, cookie auth paths, role/user seeding, logout endpoint

**Seeded Users** (all with password `Test123!`):
| Email | Role |
|---|---|
| admin@company.com | Admin |
| manager@company.com | Manager |
| employee@company.com | Employee |

**Pages**
- `/account/login` — SSR login form with cookie auth
- `/account/access-denied` — standalone access denied page
- `/admin/users` — Admin-only, lists all users + roles from `UserManager`
- `/reports` — Admin + Manager only, placeholder report cards
- `/directory` — any authenticated user, placeholder employee table

**Auth Flow**
- `Routes.razor` uses `AuthorizeRouteView` — unauthenticated users get redirected to login; authenticated users without the right role see an "Access Denied" message inline
- `NavMenu` uses `AuthorizeView` to conditionally show links based on role
- `MainLayout` top bar shows the logged-in user's name and a logout button
